payload.text: create a virtual editor for 3D worlds, where you can upload 3D objects and edit their 3D position. The objects can be images, glb, text or url to external pages. Then you can move in 3D with WASD keys for displacement, mouse control to head position, spacebar for jump and shift to run. This have to have the option to export the world in html5 with css to be uploaded in any website. Once uploaded it has to have the option to be collaboratively: Many users walking in the world at the same time, with voice chat possibility. Once rendered and uploaded the glb fils can not be downloaded (just in the editor. The tour editor have to be also in html to be used online. An example of this is the older Mozilla hubs with spoke.

**Defining the Core Features**

I'm now focusing on the essential features for this virtual world editor. I've broken down the key actions: uploading various file types like images and GLB files, editing the 3D positioning and transformations, and also implementing navigation controls such as WASD for movement and mouse for direction.


**Refining Technical Specifications**

I've been drilling down into the technical details. First-person controls are set, with space for jumping and shift for running. Exporting will be via HTML5/CSS, standalone and embeddable. I'm aiming for simulated or client-side peer-to-peer multiplayer, with Gemini Live API for voice and AI guidance. The frontend utilizes React 18, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. The 3D engine will be Three.js, via `@react-three/fiber` and `@react-three/drei`. UI and interactions will come from `react-three-gui` or a custom UI.


**Packaging the Scene**

I'm now zeroing in on the export process. The plan is a data blob with the scene and rendering script. I'll include components for the entry point, app layout, 3D scene, object toolbar, first-person controls, and Gemini integration. Specifically, GLB loaders, textures, and text elements are now set, alongside `TransformControls` for positioning, and keyboard state hooks for movement. I'm focusing on "Collaboratively" in a single file React App.

entries.description: Application metadata with required permissions for microphone and camera for 3D world interaction and voice chat.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Font configuration.
Shared TypeScript types and interfaces for the world state and objects.
Main application component managing the state of the world, UI overlays, and the 3D scene integration.
3D Scene component handling object rendering and first-person navigation.
First-person player controller with WASD, Space, Shift support.
Sidebar panel for editing object properties like position, rotation, and scale.
Gemini Live API integration for real-time voice chat and "AI World Guide" functionality.
Utility service to export the virtual world as a standalone HTML5 file.
